questions.sentence: The Observer Pattern is a fundamental design pattern used to manage state and communication between different parts of an application.
In this pattern, an object (called the 'Subject') maintains a list of its dependents (called 'Observers') and notifies them automatically of any state changes.
This is the core mechanism behind reactive frameworks like Vue and React's state updates.
This pattern promotes a 'decoupled' architecture, where different parts of the system can interact without knowing too much about each other's internal details.
Mastering the Observer Pattern helps developers understand how modern reactive UIs work and allows them to build their own custom event systems for complex scenarios.
